💸 THE VARIATION TRAP

Your builder says: "Don't worry about paperwork, I'll do it and we'll sort it out later."

What happens later: a $4,800 invoice for "variations" you thought were $200 upgrades.

Protect yourself:

✍️ EVERY change must be in writing
💰 With an agreed price BEFORE work starts
📝 Signed by both parties
📸 Photographed for your records

Don't learn this the expensive way.

1️⃣ WATERPROOFING FIRST — was it done? Do you have photo evidence?
2️⃣ GROUT LINES — even spacing, no lippage (tiles shouldn't be higher/lower than neighbours)

6️⃣ TILE LAYOUT — cut tiles should be in least visible locations, not first thing you see

1️⃣0️⃣% Base/Slab — after slab is poured

2️⃣5️⃣% Fixing — internal fitout complete

✅ Never pay for a stage that isn't ACTUALLY complete

Your builder can't demand payment ahead of completed milestones. That's the law.
